description: Add application containers to docker-compose for local end-to-end runs. Today docker-compose.dev.yml / docker-compose.test.yml define only backing infra (postgres/pgvector, redis, minio, mailhog, meilisearch, nats, redpanda, qdrant, triton, livekit) with ZERO app/service containers, so the system can't be exercised end-to-end locally.
steps: Create docker-compose.apps.yml (or extend dev compose with an `apps` profile) defining a container per app backend + key services, built from their Phase 84 Dockerfiles, depends_on the infra services, with env wired to the compose network (DATABASE_URL=postgres://...@postgres:5432, REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379, etc.).
Map the canonical ports (from Phase 83 FEAT-003 / service-discovery) so frontend proxies reach backends inside the network.
Add a `make up` / pnpm script (e.g. dev:full) that brings up infra + apps and runs the Prisma baseline migration (Phase 83 FEAT-004) before app start.
Add a tiny e2e smoke (extend scripts/smoke.ts) that hits one endpoint per app through the compose network and asserts 200/expected.
Verify `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml -f docker-compose.apps.yml up` boots the stack and the smoke passes.
acceptance_criteria: Compose defines app/service containers (not just infra)
Ports align with service-discovery; frontends reach backends in-network
A migration step runs before apps start
smoke.ts passes against the composed stack
